weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Hypertrophy Week 4 rotates in a fresh set of exercises. Read each card's cues before your first set — several movements are new. Progress by moving a little better, not by adding sets.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Box Squat — Drive Off the Box
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · sit fully, then stand up fast
patterns.load: Your competition KB · use a box LOWER than your usual box-squat height this week
cues: Use a lower box than normal — you'll sit deeper, so control the descent and own the bottom position.
Sit all the way back onto the box, pause for one count, then stand up as fast as you can.
Keep the KB tight to your chest and your heels flat — don't rock forward to get off the box.

patterns.name: KB Front-Rack Reverse Lunge
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps each leg · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: One KB held at the chest (or two in the front rack) — start lighter than your goblet squat load
cues: Step straight back, lower the back knee toward the floor, then drive through the front heel to stand.
Hold something sturdy with one hand for balance if you need it — this is about steady legs, not testing balance.
Keep your torso tall and the KB at your chest. Stop the set if your front knee caves inward.

patterns.name: Weighted Wall Sit
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 90 sec between · add ~10 sec over each previous hold
patterns.load: A 15–25 lb plate held flat across your thighs
cues: Slide down a wall until your thighs are about parallel to the floor, knees stacked over your ankles, then rest the plate flat on your thighs.
Hold the position and breathe steadily — don't hold your breath.
Heels flat, back flat against the wall. Stand up the moment your form starts to slide.

patterns.name: American Kettlebell Swing
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your competition KB (or one size up if your hinge is strong)
cues: Snap your hips forward to drive the bell all the way overhead — arms finishing by your ears. This is a hip hinge, not a squat and not an arm lift.
Brace your stomach hard at the top so your lower back doesn't arch back — stack the ribs over the hips.
If overhead bothers your shoulders, finish the bell at chest height instead. Let it swing back between your legs and snap again.

patterns.name: Overhead Backward Throw — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 throws · rest 75 sec between sets · 12 crisp throws total
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Drop into a low hinge, then explode up and throw the ball up and back over your head for distance.
All the power comes from snapping your hips open — let your arms just guide the ball.
Reset your stance completely before every throw. Quality over quantity.

patterns.name: Half-Kneeling Overhead Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each arm · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light-to-moderate DB or KB you can press for 12 clean reps
cues: Kneel on one knee and press the weight straight overhead until your arm is locked out by your ear.
Squeeze your glute and brace your stomach so your lower back doesn't arch.
Lower under control to your shoulder. Stop if you have to lean back to finish a rep.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~75% of your max-rep test · rest 75 sec between · 1-sec pause on the chest
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Same percentage as last week, but rest drops to 75 sec and every rep gets a 1-second pause on the chest — that's this week's progression.
The app shows your personal rep target. Hit it clean across all 3 sets — don't chase extra reps.
Stop a set the moment the bar stalls or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Feet-Elevated Push-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × max reps minus 2 · rest 90 sec between · leave 2 in the tank
patterns.load: Bodyweight, feet up on a bench or box — scale to fit (see cues)
cues: Scale to fit: HARDER → feet on a higher box, or a light plate on your upper back. EASIER → feet on the floor, then hands elevated on a bench, then from your knees, then hands on a wall.
Pick the version where you finish each set with about 2 clean reps still in the tank.
Lower your chest with elbows tracking back along your ribs, press to a full lockout, and keep your body in one straight line — the set ends when your hips sag.

patterns.name: DB Squeeze Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 12–15 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Two moderate DBs you can press together for 12–15 reps
cues: Lie on a bench and press two DBs straight up while squeezing them hard against each other the whole time.
The constant squeeze lights up the inner chest — hold the tension from bottom to top.
Lower slowly to your chest. Don't let the DBs drift apart.

patterns.name: Hanging Scap Pull-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · slow and controlled
patterns.load: Pull-up bar — full grip, arms straight
cues: Hang with straight arms, then pull your shoulder blades down and back to lift your body an inch — without bending your elbows.
This trains the packed-shoulder position that holds your hang time together at the comp.
Lower slowly back to a full hang. Stop the set when your grip starts to slip.

patterns.name: Kettlebell Farmer Carry
patterns.rx: 3 × 40 yards · rest 90 sec between carries
patterns.load: A heavy KB in each hand — heavy enough that grip is the limit by 40 yards
cues: Heavy carries build the grip endurance that holds your hang time at the comp.
Stand tall, shoulders packed down, walk with controlled steps — don't rush or let the bells swing.
Set the bells down the moment your grip fails. That's the end of the carry.

patterns.name: Eccentric Chin-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min between · 5-sec lower on every rep
patterns.load: Pull-up bar — step or jump to the top, then lower slowly
cues: Jump or step up so your chin is over the bar, then lower yourself as slowly as you can — aim for a 5-second descent.
The slow lower builds pulling strength and grip even if you can't do a full chin-up yet.
Keep your shoulders engaged the whole way down. Don't just drop off the bar.

patterns.name: Heavy D-Ball Deadlift (from a Squat)
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A heavy D-ball you can pick up for 10 strong reps
cues: Squat down with a flat back, hug the D-ball, then stand tall by driving through your legs and hips.
This heavy pickup builds the power that feeds your slams. D-balls only.
Lower the ball back to the floor under control. Stop the set if your back rounds.

patterns.name: D-Ball Slam — 30-Sec AMRAP
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 sec · rest 75 sec between · as many clean slams as you can
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Standing, slam as many reps as you can in 30 seconds at comp pace — this is your race-pace practice.
Full reach overhead, hips drive, slam straight down between your feet.
Keep the form crisp even as you tire. Log your slam count each round.

patterns.name: D-Ball Push Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: D-ball 10 lb (women) / 20 lb (men)
cues: Hold the D-ball at your chest, dip your knees slightly, then drive through your legs to press it overhead.
The power comes from the leg drive, not just the arms.
Lower the ball back to your chest under control and reset before the next rep.

patterns.name: Jump Rope — Speed Intervals
patterns.rx: 6 × 40 sec work / 20 sec rest · fast singles
patterns.load: Your jump rope
cues: Push the pace for 40 seconds, rest 20, repeat — six rounds. Singles only.
Stay on the balls of your feet with small, quick hops. Don't double-jump between turns.
If you trip, pick right back up — keep the rhythm going through each 40-second push.

patterns.name: Pogo Hops
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 reps each leg · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Small, springy single-leg hops in place off the ball of your foot — like a pogo stick.
Keep your knee nearly straight and let your ankle do the work; land softly and keep the hops low.
Hold a wall or rail for balance. If single-leg is too much, hop on two legs.

patterns.name: Loaded Standing Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · 2-sec hold at the top
patterns.load: Hold a DB or KB in each hand (or one at your chest) — add weight over last week if you can
cues: Stand tall, drive all the way up onto the balls of your feet, and hold the top squeeze for 2 seconds.
Lower slowly to a deep stretch off the edge of a step if you have one.
Hold a wall or rail for balance — don't let bracing turn into helping you up.

patterns.name: Squat Jump
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 jumps · rest 75 sec between sets · strong but not max
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Dip to a quarter-squat, then jump straight up as high as you can and land softly in the same spot.
Land on the balls of your feet and absorb it by bending your knees — quiet, controlled landings.
Reset fully between reps. Stop the set if your landings get sloppy.

patterns.name: Kettlebell Deadlift
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: One heavy KB between your feet, or a KB in each hand — heavy enough that 12 is challenging
cues: Hinge your hips back, grab the KB with a flat back, then stand tall by squeezing your glutes.
Drive your feet into the floor — power comes from the hips and legs, not the lower back.
Lower under control. Stop the set if your back starts to round.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 jumps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Swing your arms back, then jump forward for distance and stick the landing on both feet.
Bend your knees and hips to absorb each landing softly — this week is about clean, controlled landings.
Step back to the line and reset between every jump. Quality reps, not max effort.

patterns.name: 250m Power Repeats
patterns.rx: 3 × 250m · rest equal to your row time (1:1) · faster than your 500m race pace
patterns.load: Rower — hard, controlled pace
cues: Short and fast: row each 250m a touch quicker than your 500m race pace, then rest as long as the rep took.
Drive with your legs first, then swing back and pull — strong, smooth strokes, not frantic.
Note your split each rep and try to hold it across all three.

patterns.name: Chest-Supported DB Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Two moderate DBs — heavy enough that the last 2 reps are hard
cues: Lie face-down on an incline bench so your chest is supported, then row both DBs up to your ribs.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top and lower under control.
The bench support protects your lower back — keep your chest down the whole set.

patterns.name: 2000m Steady Row
patterns.rx: 1 × 2000m continuous · 6–8 sec/500m slower than race pace
patterns.load: Rower — conversational, steady pace
cues: One long, smooth piece — settle into a pace you could hold a conversation at.
Keep your stroke rate relaxed and your breathing even. This builds the engine under your sprint.
Ease off if your form gets sloppy; a clean steady row beats a ragged fast one.

patterns.name: Build-Up 100s
patterns.rx: 3 × 100 yards · rest as needed (full walk-back) · ramp to ~80%
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Start at an easy jog and smoothly build speed across each 100 yards — top out around 80%, never an all-out sprint.
Walk all the way back to recover before the next rep.
This is relaxed speed work to prime your legs — stay smooth, not strained.

patterns.name: 150-yd Shuttle (3 × 50)
patterns.rx: 3 × 150-yd shuttle · rest 2–3 min between · controlled turns
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Run 50 yards down and back, then down again — three lengths, 150 yards total — at a strong but controlled pace.
Slow a step before each turn, plant, and push back off — smooth turns beat hard cuts when no one's spotting you.
Rest fully between sets so each one stays quality.

patterns.name: Acceleration Strides
patterns.rx: 3 × 40 yards · rest as needed (full recovery) · 90% effort
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Drive out for 40 yards at about 90% — quick, powerful strides in a straight line.
Pump your arms and stay tall. Ease off gradually at the end rather than stopping short.
Take full recovery between reps so every stride is fast.

patterns.name: Heavy Sled Push (25 yd)
patterns.rx: 3 × 25 yards · rest 2 min between · strong, controlled
patterns.load: Comp load + 25 lb per side: 2 × 50 lb (women) / 2 × 70 lb (men)
cues: Heavier and shorter than comp distance — this builds your push strength.
Stay low with arms locked, take long steps, and drive through your toes the whole 25 yards.
Don't bend your elbows to absorb the load — push through your whole body. This is training, not a timed test.

patterns.name: Fast & Light Sled Push (20 yd)
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 yards · rest 90 sec between · max speed
patterns.load: Drop one plate per side from comp load: 1 × 25 lb (women) / 1 × 45 lb (men)
cues: Lighter sled, all about acceleration — push as fast as you can move your feet for 20 yards.
Body at about a 45° angle, arms locked, quick choppy steps to start, then lengthen your stride.
Reset and walk back between reps — every push should be fresh and fast.

patterns.name: Hand-Over-Hand Sled Pull
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 yards · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Comp load on the sled, pulled in by rope or strap
cues: Anchor yourself in a low athletic stance and pull the sled in hand-over-hand on the rope.
This trains the pulling and grip side to balance all the pushing — keep your back flat and core tight.
Pull steadily; reset your stance if you feel your lower back taking over.
